intro: Transitioning from Java to Bash shifts a developer from a highly structured, type-safe enterprise compilation ecosystem down to shell script automation directly inside the operating system. Developers trade strict compile-time safety and advanced data structures for instant command execution, low-overhead process pipeline management, and direct environment manipulation. This transition is essential when writing deployment scripts, CI/CD pipelines, or systems infrastructure orchestration utilities.

diffs.from: Statically typed, nominal class validation system ensuring type consistency before any byte code execution.
diffs.to: Untyped string-based command processing framework where context dictates whether variables act as text or numbers.

diffs.from: Verbose, class-bound boilerplate code relying on curly braces, explicit access rules, and strict expression statements.
diffs.to: Command-oriented scripting syntax that uses unique whitespace boundaries, positional arguments, and standard Unix flow operators.

diffs.from: Managed via JVM runtime tracking, running inside a shared process heap with automatic background garbage collection.
diffs.to: Process-isolated execution model where each script spawns lightweight sub-shells that automatically clean up upon termination.

diffs.from: Advanced concurrent execution runtimes, thread workers, synchronized locks, and managed virtual tasks.
diffs.to: Process-level backgrounding using simple job control tracking operators (&), standard wait checkpoints, and Unix pipeline forks.

diffs.from: Monolithic, cross-platform utility framework managing enterprise applications, database drivers, and cryptographic systems.
diffs.to: Relies entirely on standard Unix command utilities (like sed, awk, grep, and curl) interacting through text streams.

examples.title: Conditional File Validation
examples.description: Replacing Java's verbose NIO file system verification API with a concise, native Bash file testing expression.

examples.title: System Command Execution Loops
examples.description: Converting a Java system process executor loop into an idiomatic, streamlined Bash pipeline collection loop.
examples.toCode:
#!/bin/bash
ls -l | while read -r line; do
    echo "File: $line"
done

tips.title: Quote Every Variable
tips.body: Always wrap your variable expansions in double quotes (e.g., "$var") to prevent unintended script breakages caused by unexpected spaces or globbing.

tips.title: Enforce Fail-Fast Pipelines
tips.body: Add 'set -euo pipefail' to the very top of your scripts to force immediate termination upon any unexpected runtime error.

tips.title: Mind Variable Whitespace Rules
tips.body: Avoid adding arbitrary trailing spaces around variable assignments; 'var = value' will fail, whereas 'var=value' evaluates correctly.

tips.title: Ditch Try-Catch for Statuses
tips.body: Check the numeric return exit codes ($?) of executed commands instead of expecting formal exception tracking handlers.
